articles.title: 😱 84% OF PRODUCT MANAGERS FEAR THEIR PRODUCTS WILL FAIL
articles.summary: Your product manager friends are stressed. Really stressed. Atlassian surveyed 1,000+ of them - and most are convinced they're heading for failure.

articles.title: * Why pressure's higher than ever, but there's big opportunity
articles.summary: for those ready to meet it * How AI is saving teams time — and what they _still_ can't find time for * Tips for investing in experimentation and product ops, based on Atlassian's own product development experience.

articles.title: Get the full State of Product 2026 report →
9 WAYS GROWTH IS DIFFERENT IN AI COMPANIES
articles.summary: AI-native growth resets constantly, driven by prompt-box activation, big bets, and founder-led social. Product, brand, and velocity replace traditional optimizations and marketing playbooks.

articles.title: THE PURPOSE OF PROTOTYPES
articles.summary: Prototypes test risks and reveal solutions worth building. AI tools now make this faster, cheaper, and essential for product teams.

articles.title: HOW TO EFFECTIVELY PITCH CONSTRAINTS
articles.summary: Resistance to constraints stems from bias, autonomy concerns, and decision styles. Adoption improves when framed as empowerment, efficiency, or evidence-based experimentation.

articles.title: THE HIDDEN TRAP KILLING B2B PRODUCT TEAMS
articles.summary: Project stacking traps B2B teams between custom work and scalable products. Escaping it demands organizational change, not just better product management.

articles.title: 76% OF PRODUCT TEAM MEMBERS ARE EXCITED ABOUT AI. 54% AREN'T SURE HOW TO USE IT
articles.summary: Miro surveyed 2,000+ developers, PMs, and designers on AI adoption. Engineers lead in AI usage - but for others, excitement is not always keeping pace with confidence. The issue isn't technical skills, but

articles.title: leadership support for safe experimentation. Read the report
articles.summary: (includes a framework for cross-functional AI adoption [9])..

articles.title: 5 STEPS FROM FEATURES TO OUTCOME ROADMAP
articles.summary: Most roadmaps are plans, not strategy. A real roadmap builds from narratives, focuses on outcomes, and aligns with company goals.

articles.title: THE MESSAGING MATRIX: HOW TO ALIGN YOUR PRODUCT MESSAGING
articles.summary: A messaging matrix aligns product positioning, benefits, and features across teams to ensure consistent, resonant communication. Create it cross-functionally, anchor in strategy, tailor by persona, and keep it current.

articles.title: BRINGING DISCOVERY TO ENGINEERS WHO 'JUST WANT TO BUILD'
articles.summary: Discovery protects investment. Framed this way, engineers see it as risk management that saves time and prevents wasted builds.

articles.title: WHAT EVERY PARENT SHOULD KNOW ABOUT THE AI FUTURE
articles.summary: AI should amplify human potential, not replace it. Education must emphasize motivation, connection, originality, and curiosity. Parents and mentors should model balanced AI use, stressing hard work, portfolios, and relationships.

articles.title: THE UNCOMFORTABLE TRUTH ABOUT SAYING NO
articles.summary: Great product leaders say no with clarity and purpose.

articles.title: TIME FOR STRATEGIC WORK IS NOT OPTIONAL AND DOES NOT NEED TO BE EARNED
articles.summary: Focusing on execution traps leaders in reactive work.
